findings.description: The large bold title 'CONSUMER ADVISORY' in the PDF is rendered in a significantly different style in the HTML. The PDF uses an extremely large, bold, all-caps serif font centered on the page, while the HTML uses a smaller, left-aligned heading.
findings.evidence: PDF: Very large bold centered title dominating top of page. HTML: Smaller left-aligned heading with a horizontal rule underneath.

findings.description: The PDF page 1 is a centered, poster-style layout with all text centered, while the HTML renders the same content as left-aligned flowing text paragraphs.
findings.evidence: PDF: All text is center-aligned in a poster/sign format. HTML: Text is left-aligned in a standard document format.

findings.description: In the PDF, the words 'oysters, clams or mussels' are in bold italic to emphasize them. The HTML renders them in italic only (not bold), losing some emphasis distinction.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'oysters, clams or mussels' in bold italic. HTML: 'oysters, clams or mussels' in italic only.

findings.description: The body text in the PDF page 1 is all bold, large font size, while the HTML renders it in normal weight, smaller body text.
findings.evidence: PDF: Entire advisory text is in large bold font. HTML: Advisory text is in normal weight, standard body size.

findings.description: The PDF separates the advisory text and the legal/penalty text across pages 1-3, with clear visual separation. The HTML consolidates this content onto fewer visual sections but maintains reading order.
findings.evidence: PDF: Content spans pages 1-3 with the 'Shellfish Sanitation Section' and failure-to-post warning on separate pages. HTML: All content is consolidated on what appears as one continuous page.

findings.description: The failure-to-post warning paragraph is in bold in the PDF (page 3) but appears in regular weight in the HTML version.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3: Bold text for the failure-to-post warning. HTML: Same text in normal weight.
